prompt:
Use case: identity-preserve
Asset type: runtime tactics unit sprite
Primary request: regenerate the House Marshal as a distinct Order command unit using the concept silhouette and the approved Squire Operative as the color and rendering anchor
Subject: a heavily armored Order officer with a closed steel helmet from the concept, broad pauldrons, commanding stance, long relic rifle, heavier chest armor, and a dark wine cape or tabard accent
Style/medium: moody post-apocalyptic tactics sprite illustration with stronger pixel clustering, chunkier readable shapes, and a painterly pixel-art-inspired finish that survives downscaling cleanly
Composition/framing: single full-body subject, centered, facing slightly left, grounded battlefield-ready stance, transparent background, tight sprite bounds
Lighting/mood: warm bunker light with restrained contrast, no cold aquatic glow, no teal rim-lighting
Color palette: vibrant dark wine red, warm parchment ivory, aged brass, weathered iron black
Constraints: preserve the helmeted officer silhouette from the concept, explicitly no hood, make the unit visibly heavier and more authoritative than the Squire Operative, keep the Order faction material treatment from the approved Squire anchor, rendered around 106px tall in game, prioritize large readable pixel-like shapes and reduced micro-detail so the sprite holds up at tactical scale, no floor, no scenery, no text
Avoid: hood, myrmidon-style lighting, cool cyan glow, fantasy knight styling, photorealism, anime gloss, cinematic background, fine noisy texture, oversized empty padding, extra characters, extra props
